theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Industrials (+0.64%) and Consumer Discretionary (+0.28%) led the market today, while Consumer Staples (-0.59%) and Energy (-0.36%) brought up the rear. Performance didn't cluster cleanly by style — growth averaged +0.01%, defensives -0.16%, and value/cyclicals +0.06% — more a stock-by-stock market than a single broad theme. That shows up at the index level too: the tech-heavy Nasdaq gained +0.20% while the blue-chip Dow lagged at -1.09%. Small caps underperformed as well, with the Russell 2000 at -0.88%, hinting the move favored large, established balance sheets over smaller, more rate-sensitive companies. The VIX fell 1.01% to 16.71, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 1.17% to $4,119.70, crude oil was little changed at $72.92, and bitcoin gained 1.02% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
